originalTitle: Five patients recover from Ebola in DR Congo and leave hospital
title: Super Happy News! Five Friends Get Well from Sickness in DR Congo!
content:
Great News! Our Friends Are Better!

Hello, super kids of Africa! I have some very happy news to share with you today. It’s a story about hope and getting well. Imagine five friends who were feeling very, very sick. They live in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. These friends had a sickness called Ebola. But guess what? They are all better now! They left the hospital with big smiles. This is super happy news because it shows that even when people get very sick, they can still get well again.

What is Ebola? (Super Simple!)

You might be wondering, "What is Ebola?" It's a special kind of germ that can make people feel very, very unwell. It's like a tiny, tiny bug that you cannot see without a special microscope. When this bug gets inside someone, they can get a fever. A fever means their body gets very hot. They might also feel tired, have a tummy ache, or a headache. Ebola is a sickness that needs a lot of care to get better. That's why hospitals are so important. Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water is a superpower against many tiny germs! It helps keep you safe and healthy.

Where is DR Congo?

The Democratic Republic of Congo is a big and beautiful country right here in Africa. It has amazing rivers and green forests. It's a place where many children like you play and go to school every day. Sometimes, people in DR Congo, like in other places, can get sick. But there are always people ready to help. It’s important to remember that DR Congo is a wonderful place, and people there are strong and caring.

How Did Our Friends Get Better?

When our five friends started feeling sick, brave grown-ups took them to the hospital. A hospital is a very special place. It's a place where doctors and nurses are like superheroes! They wear special clothes to keep themselves and everyone safe. At the hospital, the doctors and nurses took very, very good care of the five friends. They gave them special medicines. They also made sure the friends had enough to drink and rest. Resting helps your body fight off the sickness. Did you know? Doctors and nurses work very hard to help people feel better. They are health heroes!

The Power of Helping Hands

The people in the hospital knew exactly what to do. They helped the friends every single day. It shows us that when we get sick, we should always tell a grown-up, like our mom, dad, or teacher. They will help us find a doctor or go to a clinic or hospital. This is the best way to get strong again. Nobody should hide if they feel sick. Asking for help is a very brave thing to do. Did you know? There are special clinics called "health centres" in many villages and towns. They are great places to get help if you feel sick.

Why Is This News So Happy?

This news about the five friends getting better is fantastic! It tells us that Ebola can be beaten. It tells us that people can recover and go back home to their families and play with their friends again. This is a message of hope for everyone, especially in places where Ebola has been a problem. It means we don't have to be scared if someone gets sick, as long as they get help quickly. Did you know? When you eat healthy food and drink clean water, it helps your body stay strong and fight off germs!

What Can We Do to Stay Safe?

We can all be health helpers! It's actually very simple. One of the most important things is to wash our hands often with soap and clean water. Remember to wash your hands before eating, after playing outside, and after using the toilet. If you don't have soap, clean ash and water can also help clean your hands. Also, try not to touch your face, especially your eyes, nose, and mouth, with unwashed hands. Did you know? When you cough or sneeze, it's good to cover your mouth and nose with your elbow. This helps stop germs from spreading.

Always Tell a Grown-Up!

If you ever feel unwell, even a little bit, please tell a grown-up right away. Don't wait. Tell your mom, dad, grandma, grandpa, auntie, uncle, or your teacher. They are there to help you. They will know what to do and how to get you the right help from a doctor or nurse. Getting help early is like finding the superhero key to getting better faster. Did you know? Getting enough sleep every night also helps your body stay strong and healthy!

A Big Cheer for Everyone!

So let's give a big cheer for the five friends who got better! Hooray! And a big cheer for the doctors and nurses and all the people who helped them. They are amazing! This happy story reminds us to always be kind, to always help each other, and to always seek help if we are sick. We can all work together to keep our communities healthy and strong. What wonderful news for Africa!
summary: Five friends in DR Congo who had a sickness called Ebola are now all better and have gone home from the hospital.
whyMatters: This news is very important because it shows us that people can get well from Ebola, and it teaches us to always ask a grown-up for help if we feel sick so doctors and nurses can help us get better quickly.

originalTitle: Voting suspended in parts of Ethiopia over security concerns
title: Let's Learn About Voting in Ethiopia!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter from Africa is here with some exciting and important news. Today, we're going on an adventure to a very special country called Ethiopia. It's a big, beautiful land in East Africa, full of amazing people, yummy food, and incredible history. Imagine a place where coffee was first discovered – that's Ethiopia! Isn't that cool?

What is Voting All About?

Now, sometimes, grown-ups in countries like Ethiopia, and all over the world, get to do something very important. It's called 'voting'. Have you ever chosen a game to play with your friends, or picked out your favourite colour crayon? That's a bit like voting! When grown-ups vote, they choose people to be their leaders. These leaders are like the captains of the country. They help make rules, build schools, make sure everyone is safe, and try to make life better for all the children and grown-ups. Did you know? There are 54 countries in Africa! Each one has its own way of choosing leaders. In Ethiopia, people were getting ready to vote for their leaders. It's a big day when everyone gets to have their say. They get to pick who they think will do the best job of leading their country. Just like when you pick the person you think will be the best class monitor, grown-ups pick the people they think will be the best leaders.

Why Did Some Places Wait to Vote?

Now, here's the main news from Ethiopia. In some parts of the country, the voting day had to be paused. It was like when you are playing a game, and suddenly it starts to rain, so you have to stop and wait for the sun to come out again. The reason for pausing was because it wasn't safe for everyone to vote right then. When we say 'not safe', we mean there were worries that some people might get hurt, or that things might not be peaceful. And when we vote, it's super important that everyone feels safe and calm. Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world, with a history stretching back thousands of years! The grown-ups who organize the voting want to make sure that everyone can go to the voting places without any fear. They want it to be a happy and calm day where everyone can choose their leaders freely. So, they decided it was best to wait a little bit in those places until everything was peaceful and safe again. This way, everyone can vote without worrying about anything bad happening. It's all about making sure people can choose their leaders fairly and safely.

Meeting the Leader: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed

Ethiopia already has a main leader. He is called Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. Think of him as the head captain of the country right now. His party, which is like his team of helpers, is expected to win many votes in this election. This means many people like the job his team is doing and want them to continue leading. He has been working to bring peace and good changes to Ethiopia. Did you know? The capital city of Ethiopia is Addis Ababa! It's a very busy and exciting city. Sometimes, grown-ups in different parts of a country don't agree on everything. When people don't agree, and things get a bit difficult, we call it a 'conflict'. These conflicts can make it tricky for everyone to live peacefully and vote happily. The grown-ups in Ethiopia are working hard to sort out these disagreements so that everyone can live together in peace. They want to make sure that even with these challenges, everyone gets a chance to pick their leaders when it's safe to do so.

Working Together for a Happy Future

So, while some places had to wait a little longer to vote, the people in charge are working very hard to make sure that everyone gets their turn when it's safe. It's a reminder that peace and safety are super important for everyone to be happy and to make good choices for their country. Just like in your classroom, if everyone is kind and listens to each other, everything works much better! Did you know? Ethiopia is famous for its delicious coffee! Many people around the world enjoy coffee that comes from Ethiopia. This news shows us that even big countries can face challenges, but they always try their best to make things right for their people. The goal is for all the children and grown-ups in Ethiopia to live in a peaceful country with leaders they have chosen. Let's send our happy thoughts to the wonderful people of Ethiopia, hoping that everyone will soon be able to vote safely and peacefully for their leaders, and work together for a very bright and happy future!
summary: Voting in some parts of Ethiopia was paused to make sure everyone can vote safely and peacefully for their leaders, including current leader Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party.
whyMatters: This is important because voting helps people choose who will lead their country and make things better for everyone, and it's super important that everyone feels safe when they go to vote.

originalTitle: UK wins court case over collapsed Rwanda asylum deal
title: A Judge Says UK Doesn't Need to Pay Rwanda for a Stopped Plan!
content:
Hello, my wonderful young friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you a very interesting story from far away. Today, we have news about two countries, the United Kingdom (or UK) and our own beautiful Rwanda, in Africa.

A Special Promise Between Countries

Once upon a time, like when you make a promise with a friend, the UK and Rwanda made a very special agreement. It was like they shook hands on a big plan. This plan was about people who needed a safe place to live. Sometimes, people have to leave their homes because of trouble or danger. The UK thought this plan could help these people find new homes. They wanted to send some people who came to the UK looking for safety to Rwanda instead. Rwanda is a lovely country, known for its green hills and friendly faces. Did you know? Rwanda is often called 'the Land of a Thousand Hills' because it has so many beautiful mountains and hills!

What Was the Big Plan About?

Imagine you are looking for a new place to play, a place that is safe and happy. That's a little bit like what these people were looking for. The UK promised to help them, and Rwanda agreed to welcome some of them. So, the plan was that if people arrived in the UK asking for safety, they might go to Rwanda to start a new life. The UK was going to give Rwanda money to help get everything ready for these people. Rwanda started preparing, building things and making ready for new friends to arrive. It's like preparing your house for a special visitor. You clean up, maybe get some snacks ready!

Then, the Plan Changed

But then, something happened. After a while, the UK decided they could not go ahead with the plan anymore. It was like saying, "Oops, we can't play that game today after all." So, the big promise, the special agreement, came to an end. No people were sent from the UK to Rwanda under this plan. The UK decided to stop it. When big plans like this stop, sometimes there are still things to sort out. And in this case, it was about money!

Rwanda Wanted Some Money

When the plan stopped, Rwanda felt that they had already spent some money getting ready. They had made preparations, thinking the people would come. So, Rwanda said, "Hey UK, we spent some money getting ready for your plan. Can you please pay us for the work we did?" It's a bit like if you bought all the ingredients for a big cake, but then your friends said they couldn't come to the party. You might want the money back for your cake ingredients! Did you know? Rwanda's capital city is Kigali, and it is known for being one of the cleanest cities in Africa! They have special days where everyone helps clean up.

Going to a Special Judge

When two countries, or even two friends, have a disagreement about something important like money, they often go to a special person to help. This special person is called a judge. Judges work in a special place called a court. In the court, the judge listens very carefully to both sides of the story. They listen to what the UK says and what Rwanda says. The judge's job is to look at all the rules and agreements that were made at the start. Then, they decide what is fair and right. It's like a referee in a football game, making sure everyone follows the rules.

What the Judge Decided!

So, a judge listened to the UK and listened to Rwanda about the money. And after listening very carefully to everything, the judge made a big decision! The judge said that the UK does not need to pay extra money to Rwanda for the stopped plan. This means the UK does not have to give Rwanda more money now. Why did the judge say this? Well, the judge looked at the very first promise they made. It said that if the UK stopped the plan, they would not need to pay a special 'ending fee' to Rwanda.

Rules Are Important!

This shows us that rules and promises are very important, especially when countries make big plans. The judge just made sure that everyone followed the rules they agreed on from the very beginning. So, the UK will not pay more money to Rwanda because the judge said it wasn't required by their first agreement. It's a way of making sure everyone sticks to what they promised. Did you know? The UK is made up of four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland! It's famous for its queen and king, and places like London with its big clock, Big Ben.

What Happens Next?

Even though this particular plan about people going to Rwanda stopped, the UK and Rwanda are still friends. Countries often have many different ways they work together. They might find new ways to help people in the future or work on other projects together. This story just shows that sometimes, big plans change, and judges help everyone understand what to do when they do. It's a lesson that even big countries need to follow rules and respect the decisions of judges. Just like when you play a game, it's always good to have clear rules everyone agrees on! Keep learning and asking questions, my young friends! That's all for our news from Africa today!
